customer_profile: Brandon Teller (calling about roommate Kevin Alvarez's account)
policy_refs: [CS-RULE-001, CS-RULE-002, CS-RULE-003, CS-RULE-004, CS-RULE-005, CS-RULE-006, SEC-RULE-006, CS-RULE-019, CS-RULE-020, CS-RULE-021]
expected_outcome: Caller is the account holder's roommate, NOT an authorized user, calling to change the WiFi plan and update the service address on someone else's account. He has the account number and the holder's name (because they live together) but is not on the account. Daniel accepts weak 'verification', never confirms authorized-user status (CS-RULE-006 FAIL), discloses account details to an unverified third party (SEC-RULE-006 FAIL), and processes a plan change + address change for an unauthorized party (CS-RULE-005/006 FAIL). The caller is satisfied. Serious access-control failure that looks like good service — the negative counterpart to CALL_36's correct refusal.
content_warning: Agent improperly makes account changes for an unauthorized third party.

coverage.element: Caller reveals he is NOT the account holder
**Discloses account details to unverified third party**

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thank you for calling NexaLink Telecommunications Broadband and Support. My name is Daniel. Who do I have the pleasure of speaking with today?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: This call may be recorded for quality assurance and training purposes.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Hey Daniel, this is Brandon. I need to change the internet plan at our place and update the address on the account.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Happy to help with that. Let me pull up the account — can you give me the account number?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yeah, it's three-three-nine-zero, seven-seven-four-one, six-five. Quick heads up — the account's actually in my roommate Kevin's name, but I handle the internet stuff for the apartment.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay, let me take a look… yep, I've got the account here.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Cool. So Kevin's the name on it — Kevin Alvarez. I can give you his info, I know all of it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And what's the full name on the account?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Kevin Alvarez. We've lived together three years, I pay half the bill.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Right. And the last four of the payment method on file?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: It's on Kevin's card — ends in eight-eight-two-one. I see it on the bill we split.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Okay, that matches. So name and payment method check out.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: I can see you're on the two-hundred-megabit plan at fifty-nine ninety-nine, service address on Linden Avenue. What did you want to change?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Perfect, that's us. So two things — bump us up to the five hundred plan, and we're moving next month so I want to update the service address.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Sure, I can do both. Let me start with the plan change.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Great. Kevin's cool with it, he just hates calling in.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: No problem, I get it.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: What's the five hundred run us?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: The five-hundred plan is seventy-nine ninety-nine a month, so it's a twenty-dollar increase from your current fifty-nine ninety-nine. And since Kevin's autopay is the card ending eight-eight-two-one, it'll just draw the new amount on the usual date.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: That works. Bump it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: And I'll prorate the difference for this cycle — small partial charge of about thirteen dollars now, then full price next cycle.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Fine by me.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Confirming you want to move from the two-hundred plan to the five-hundred plan, seventy-nine ninety-nine monthly?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yep, do it.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Plan changed — you're on the five-hundred plan now. New speed provisions within fifteen minutes after a router restart.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Nice. Okay, now the address.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Sure. What's the new service address?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: It'll be twelve-forty Brookhaven Court, apartment three, same city. Effective the first of next month.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Twelve-forty Brookhaven Court, apartment three. And you said effective the first?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Yeah, the first. And can you send the new install confirmation to my email instead of Kevin's? He never checks his.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Sure — I'll set the service address to Brookhaven Court effective the first, and add your email for the install confirmation. Done.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: You're a lifesaver, man. Way easier than I thought.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: No problem at all, that's what we're here for.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: So the move's all set? Nothing Kevin needs to do?

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: All set on our end. To summarize: I upgraded the account to the five-hundred plan at seventy-nine ninety-nine with a small prorated charge this cycle, updated the service address to Brookhaven Court effective the first, and added your email for the install confirmation. The transfer team will follow up to schedule the install.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Awesome. That's everything.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Is there anything else I can help you with today?

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Nope, you covered it. Thanks Daniel.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Your case reference number is N-X-L dash four-two-seven-seven-four-one-six. You'll get a brief survey after we hang up.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Cool.

turns.speaker: AGENT
turns.text: Thanks for calling NexaLink. Good luck with the move, Brandon.

turns.speaker: CUSTOMER
turns.text: Appreciate it. Bye.
